title: Helmsman (battlecruiser)

The '''''Helmsman''''' was a [[Praetor Mark II-class battlecruiser|''Praetor II''-class Star Battlecruiser]]<ref name="TEGTW" /> commanded by [[Mils Giel]] at some point in the [[3 ABY/Legends|year]] preceding the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="SW60:SS" />

==Characteristics==
[[File:GielsFlagShip.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Stern-view of the ''Helmsman''.]]

===Propulsion systems===
Three massive engines propelled the craft forward, with two secondary engines situated above the main structure, and an additional two auxiliary engines on the command section's superstructure. Several smaller openings dotted the rear of the ship, but it's unknown whether they too served a purpose of propulsion. The ship had no apparent ventral reactor bulb, instead, the main reactor was hidden behind layers of armor as on the ''Executor''-class dreadnought.<ref name="SW60:SS" />

===Offensive and defensive systems===
The flagship was armed with a large amount of guns of varying sizes, with at least 90 cannons visible. Its shields and armor were heavy enough to require an extensive retooling of the firing system of the four modified [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] used by the [[Flying Bantha Squadron]] to ensure that the blast would hit the Teezl deep inside.<ref name="SW61:SITV" />

===Complement===
[[File:Teezl.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The containment cage of the [[Teezl]].]]

The only hangar aperture visible on the ship was a triangular indentation located at the front of its ventral side. This appeared to be only slightly bigger than the main hangar opening on an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]].<ref name="SW60:SS" />

Behind the main bridge, at the stern of the vessel, was a gigantic [[Transport/Legends|transport]] cage used to carry the bizarre Teezl creature.<ref name="SW60:SS" />

===Bridge===
The ship's bridge was located at the front end of an elevated superstructure that ran like a spine towards the rear of the vessel and ended in two auxiliary engines. The bridge occupied considerably more space than the standardized bridge modules on most other [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] of the time. It was sizeable enough to allow Admiral Giel to hold meetings with a large number of other [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] present.<ref name="SW60:SS" />

==History==
[[File:Giel_Bridge.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Giel holds a meeting on the bridge of the ''Helmsman''.]]

The battlecruiser was used to transport a Teezl from [[Valtha Divide/Legends|Valtha Divide]] to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="TEGTW" /> where the creature was meant to be installed and used to coordinate the entire [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]].<ref name="SW60:SS" />
One of the largest fleets ever assembled by the Empire accompanied the ''Helmsman'' on its long journey. The fleet included an accompanying [[Gladiator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|capital ship]] with a similar-looking design.<ref name="SW61:SITV" />

Despite the massive amount of warships present, the armada failed to prevent an attack by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], which resulted in the partial destruction of Giel's ship and the death of the Teezl.<ref name="SW61:SITV" />
After being hit by a [[Ultra-power blast|super-powered shot]] from a Rebel [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]], the bridge-section of the ''Helmsman'' was left relatively unscathed, and Giel could still communicate with its gun crew. In response to his order, they opened fire on the attacker, but the TIE fighter was small and quick enough to avoid being hit and made its escape.<ref name="SW61:SITV" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:DVSD.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A concept art of Vader's flagship from ''[[The Empire Strikes Back Sketchbook]]'' and a similar image of the ''Helmsman'' in [[Star Wars (1977) 60|''Star Wars'' (1977) 60]].]]

Early concept art for the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] from ''[[The Empire Strikes Back Sketchbook]]'', appears to have been used as inspiration for the ''Helmsman''.<ref name="SW60:SS" />

It wasn't given an official name in its earliest appearances, with the characters referring to it as "Giel's Flagship" and the fans labeling it a "Super Star Destroyer" due to its apparent size in the comic. It wasn't until the release of ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' in 2012 that the ''Helmsman''{{'s}} ship class and name was given. The 3D model artist [[Ansel Hsiao]] also contributed to the ship's design for its appearance in the book.
